title: Phase 0 dispatched as a single lead, not a parallel fan-out
rationale: Ovyro's repo was empty at kickoff (only projectspec.html + tooling). The spec's 7 phases look parallelizable but are not: P1 needs P0, P2 needs P1 (leads FK listings), P4 needs P2 (leads carry meta_event_id), P5 needs P4. More importantly, any parallel builder on an empty repo scaffolds its own package.json/tsconfig/next.config, guaranteeing merge conflicts on the exact files that define the build. Dispatched ONE lead for OVYRO-34b8 with explicit instruction to land the scaffold as a single commit BEFORE fanning out builders onto disjoint areas (drizzle schema / auth / worker+queue / infra+seed). Fan-out becomes safe only once the shared scaffold exists.

description: Builder sessions spawned in nested worktrees inherit the CANONICAL repo root .claude/settings.local.json coordinator hooks: those PreToolUse hooks deny Write/Edit unconditionally and deny most file-creating Bash whenever OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME is non-empty, so builder-scaffold (OVYRO-4a5c) could not create any file in its own worktree. Denial text: 'coordinator agents cannot modify files'. The worktree-local builder hooks (path-boundary only) are correct; the leak is settings scope, not the overlay.
resolution: Scope coordinator hooks to the coordinator: each hook guard in the canonical settings.local.json should exit 0 unless OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME equals 'coordinator' (currently it exits 0 only when the var is empty). Alternatively spawn builders so their Claude session loads only the worktree .claude settings. Reported via urgent mail msg-n8f45jl73gi9 to coordinator; lead-foundation was already terminal.

title: Halted Ovyro Phase 0 dispatch pending hook-scope fix; do not re-dispatch builders until fixed
rationale: Verified at the settings file itself, not just from the builder's report: .claude/settings.local.json at the canonical repo root carries coordinator-profile PreToolUse deny hooks (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit/mutating-Bash) guarded only by '[ -z $OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME ] && exit 0', which skips enforcement only for empty (human) sessions. Overstory worktrees are nested inside the repo, so every spawned agent inherits this root settings file and no builder can create a file. Two agents burned producing zero code. Re-dispatching builders before the guard is rescoped to '[ $OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME != coordinator ] && exit 0' will fail identically every time — the correct coordinator response is to halt dispatch and escalate to the human, not to retry or to seek hook bypasses. Path-boundary hooks (worktree-scoped) are correct and must be left intact. Planning state is preserved and needs no rework: OVYRO-34b8 epic, OVYRO-4a5c scaffold spec, phases 1-6 dependency-chained, group ovyro-v1-build.

content: The canonical .claude/settings.local.json PreToolUse hooks were rescoped (as of 2026-07-21 23:32) from unconditional coordinator denies to path-boundary-only checks, unblocking builder agents in worktrees - mx-e15887 no longer reproduces. One residual sharp edge: the Bash path-boundary hook false-positives on 'git commit -F -' with a heredoc, rejecting it as a boundary violation. Use repeated -m flags for multi-paragraph commit messages instead.

description: 'ov mail send --body' with a long body is rejected by the PreToolUse path-boundary hook with 'Bash path boundary violation: command targets a path outside your worktree', even when the body contains no paths at all and the identical content splits into chunks that each send fine. Bodies around 1100 chars passed; around 2400 chars and above failed consistently. The message is misleading — it reads as a scope violation when it is really command-length driven, so agents waste turns hunting for a nonexistent bad path.
resolution: Split long reports into several sequential 'ov mail send' calls (roughly 1000 chars or less each), then send a short terminal worker_done that references them. Do not try to fix it by rewording paths out of the body — that is not the trigger. Note 'ov mail send' has no --body-file flag, so a file-based workaround is unavailable.

description: Coordinator sd create and ov mail commands with a multi-LINE --desc/--body value are rejected with the misleading coordinator hook error 'coordinator agents cannot modify files — this command is not allowed', even though the coordinator is allowed to run sd/ov. Cause: the coordinator PreToolUse Bash hook extracts and pattern-matches the command line-by-line; embedded newlines in a quoted argument make an interior line parse as a standalone command that trips a block pattern. Length is NOT the trigger (2200-char single-line filler passes); newlines are.
resolution: Always pass sd create --desc and ov mail --body as a SINGLE line (no embedded newlines). Use '. ' sentence breaks instead of line breaks. Verified: same multi-line text that errors becomes an instant success when flattened to one line.

title: Phase 1 run as compressed coordinator/lead with direct builders, not a spawned lead
rationale: Two lead agents this session (lead-foundation and the wave-1 lead) exited after a single turn without terminal mail, orphaning their builders. Meanwhile the direct-builder-plus-coordinator-verify pattern completed Phase 0 cleanly through 4 builders. For Phase 1 the coordinator acts as combined coordinator/lead: dispatch a shared listings-core builder first (status machine, Zod schemas, slug, DTO types in src/lib/listings), merge it, then fan out 4 disjoint wave-2 builders (seller CRUD, R2 media, public browse/SSR, admin moderation) that import core. This is the sanctioned low-instability compression, chosen on observed lead failures, not a hierarchy bypass. Coordinator self-verifies each branch (gates + scope + domain rules) before merge since no lead signs off.

name: Per-phase integration-verification builder catches cross-wave defects
description: After fanning out N parallel builders on disjoint scopes and merging each (self-verified in isolation), dispatch ONE final integration-verification builder that branches from the fully-merged main, runs a fresh install plus full gates plus a real-service end-to-end lifecycle, and fixes small integration defects forward. Both Phase 0.4 and Phase 1.5 justified it: P1.5 caught worker/queue.integration.test.ts asserting a stale 3-queue registry that only fails once ALL waves are on main (the media wave added media-processing; the AI conflict-merge kept the registry correct but no one updated this cross-cutting assertion). Per-slice verification structurally cannot catch this. Instruct the verifier to report credential-blocked items (R2, Resend) honestly rather than fake them, and to state any services it starts on the machine.

Phase 2 merged main could not boot: I specced the inquiry route as /api/listings/[id]/leads while the existing public route was /api/listings/[slug]. Next.js forbids sibling dynamic route segments with DIFFERENT param names, so the router 500s every request at runtime. next build + typecheck + lint + test ALL PASS and even list both routes — only next start (a live boot) rejects it. Per-slice builder verification and all four gates missed it; only the phase integration-verification builder booting a real server caught it.
resolution: When specifying a new dynamic route under an existing segment, ALWAYS reuse the existing param name (grep src/app for the sibling [param] before writing the issue). Fixed by unifying the public listings subtree on [slug]. LESSON for coordinator: match existing route conventions in issue specs; and the phase verification builder MUST boot a real server (next start), not just run next build, because build-time checks do not validate the runtime router.
